name: Rich Lemon Brownies
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Melt butter and chocolate in a saucepan or double boiler over simmering water, stirring occasionally until smooth. Allow mixture to cool completely. Add sugar and eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition. Stir in flour and vanilla, blending until just combined. Pour batter into a greased and floured 9 x 13 inch pan. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes, or until the edges pull away from the sides of the pan but the center is still moist. Remove from oven and let cool slightly.
For the lemon frosting: Melt butter and chocolate in a saucepan or double boiler over simmering water, stirring occasionally until smooth. Let mixture cool slightly. Gradually add confectioners' sugar while whisking to achieve desired consistency. Beat in egg, vanilla, and lemon juice, mixing well. Fold in chopped pecans if desired. Frosting will harden as it cools. If a thicker frosting is preferred, add more confectioners' sugar. Once the brownies have cooled, frost with lemon glaze and cut into 1 1/2 x 2 inch bars.
